@Vyor In which country ?
@N how do you prosecute?

@Vyor I forgot to add "usually". Usually, in an online harassment incident, when it came to be real life problem, the crime already taken place, the victim already be harmed. (Like, after you doxxed people, the Internet already knew the victim's identity. When you falsely accuse somebody for something they didn't do or any other type of harrasment related to one's reputations,  their reputations already got ruined.) Turning online harassment into a crime is a precaution for these cases.
@N why is it already too late?

@Vyor Problem is: It came from online first. Online threatening and doxxing can be considered online harrasment, what happened next is the result of said process. Usually, when you deal with the real life part, it is already too late.
@N which would be taking it to real life.
@Vyor There are many reasons why online harassment can lead to deaths. One example: doxxing. Another one: ruining reputations, falseful accusations, lying about someone, etc...
@Force Edge 200 online harassment isn't a crime in most countries. Just turn off the fucking Twitter and walk away. Oh look, no more harassment.

@masch are you sure about these numbers? Those sound like official numbers, like you get hired for 37.5 hours per week, for x weeks per year. But there is a real expectation at a lot of companies that you will work way longer than that. Some companies (and even smaller places like restaurants) won't hire foreigners because they don't have this mentality ingrained into them.

@Force Edge 200 they're getting more and more aware about it but some people still don't take this issue seriously due to so many online trolls in Japan often fake their mental condition for clout. Even though there was an online troll that took things seriously and committed crime in Akihabara years ago

@Riki Shikato It's not helped that Japan has been really slow on the awareness of mental health until recently. Like for a while (and I think it still is) online harassment isn't considered a crime unless it gets physical (which normally by that time it's too late).

@masch yes, Japan isn't at the top. But this issue kept being brought up because it often involves depression to the point of self-end. But most (if not all) black companies don't give a flying fucks about their employees' mental health and kept them slaving in the office. They kept avoiding this issue by saying "It's for the economy, bruh. You gotta understand that."

Japan isn’t even in the top for work time and according to the OECD slightly below the OECD average with 1710 work hours/year. In contrast the US is slightly above with 1780 work hours/year. It gets mentioned in Japanese media because it is seen as an issue that needs to be tackled. Just like violent crime gets mentioned in US media all the time although violent crime rates have been constantly decreasing over several decades. Media coverage just means it’s an issue, but not that the issue is necessarily increasing or worse or the worst compared to other countries.

Sushi is very versatile. 


First, you can have sushi-wearing girls put them on and suddenly become chibi, or have girls wearing sushi flashing those cute grins, or have girls stealing the protagonist's sushi and eating them like, "Haha, got your sushi!" That's just way too cute! Also, boys with sushi! I really like when their sushi have that suspicious looking gleam, and it's amazing how it can look really cool or just be a joke. I really like how it can fulfill all those abstract needs. Being able to switch up the styles and type of fish based on your taste is a lot of fun, too! It's actually so much fun! You have those sake sushi, or the tekka sushi, everything! It's like you're enjoying all these kinds of sushi at a buffet. I really want Luna to become one or Marine to become one on to replace her body. We really need sushi to become a thing in hololive and start selling them for HoloComi. Don't. You. Think. We. Really. I've said this before but I'll say it again because I really like it.
1:17 the "White Death" is a reference to a Finnish sniper during the Winter War between Finland and the Soviet Union. He killed 505 Soviet soldiers during the war. His name was Simo Häyhä, nicknamed the "White Death". If you listen carefully you can hear Fubuki say his name. I actually noticed this after watching with Indonesian subtitle, where Simo Häyhä is written there instead of White Death.

第一問の解答(俺の)

因数分解程度の問題を解けないと一般社会に出られないから。
特化型分野を除く(スポーツ選手、音楽家、画家等)

社会で因数分解を使うことはほぼ無いだろうけれど、一定以上の計算力は求められる。そのため、計算力を担うために因数分解を含む、その他色々な計算を学んでいる。

因数分解だけでなく、「何故数学でこんな難しい問題を！」と思うのなら、それは至って簡単で高校に入り、大学に行き、いい会社に勤めるためである。(一般論)

So Towa's hands got stucked on her face. The door bell rings so Towa tries to anwser it but fails and got into more mess. Meanwhile Miko was hosting a game show with other members. Okayu came and help towa get out of her mess and urge her to answer the door. It is revealed that towa is in the game show Miko is hosting so Towa is confused. It is possible that Miko planned the whole situation like she did with Sora and Mel. The last question is probably Miko asking the competitors from which door would Towa-sama come out and the answer is the door with the circle.
Edit: also the sound of the door bell is from contestants ringing the bells to answer questions.

That's what i thought anyway.

@kebien6020 another not-quite-pun but additional note, subaru sneezed after her name was mentioned, and in jp (and other) culture, sneezing once means someone is thinking/talking about you (twice is badmouthing)
Can I ask How many times you have watched the video ? 😂 That was some very understandable explanation right there
I think that it was saying everything came full circle, with the door being opened and connecting to the game show

to further explain the joke @kebien6020 already explained
japanese have a saying "neko no te mo karitai" which quite literally means "we're even willing to borrow a cat's help(ing hand)" because they are so busy
It could also be that the answer to Miko's question was answering the door

Also, a couple of puns.
- Okayu offers Towa a cat's hand as help. Which is a Japanese expression, but then she literally helps her using cats. Translated as "cater to your needs".
- Sora = Sky. Both Tokino Sora and Yozora Mel have that in their names. When they land they point that Oozora Subaru also has that. Translated as "having soar in their name".

This episode was either just a random story or some high level meme way above my head.
In any case, I loved it!

Doorbell ringing: not actually the doorbell, but game show noises. 
Tokino 'Sora', Yo'zora' Mel and Oo'zora' Subaru all have the kanji for sky (sora) in their names, which is what Mel replies when Sora asks why they're falling, and they mention Subaru after.
Neko no te mo karitai - a Japanese saying that translates roughly to "I would even borrow a cat's paw", as in "I'm so busy even a cat lending a paw would help". Here it's used literally instead of figuratively.
The last piece is the official comment that contains the third question: Has Tokoyami Towa ever been saved by a cat? ○ for yes, × for no.
That's Hologra in a nutshell xD

Brief History Lesson for those who don’t know:

Regarding what happens at 0:27 :

“Perry” and “The Black Ships” are a reference to when Commodore Matthew Perry lead a fleet of gunboats as part of the first official American delegation to Japan in 1853 in order to force Japan to open up its ports to American (and ultimately European) trade and commerce. His expedition to Japan is one of the events which kickstarted the end of isolationist Japan and ultimately the collapse of the Tokugawa Shogunate, as well as began a decades-long period where Japan fell under the influence of various Western countries and was forced to make various economic concessions.
I laughed so hard when I saw the reference, glad to have a fellow fan!

At last, Iroha has finally made her first hologra appearance! I'm really really happy for her! The first day was already filled with chaos for our brave and strong nin-ni- I mean, samurai but for sure it looks like it's a great start for her nonetheless.. I guess.

The fight between Ojou-sama at the beginning? I never thought I'd see the day. Coffee spilled all over my lap by surprise. I've been waiting for this moment to finally happen since I first saw edoman's animation months ago, and just like the fan animation, Iroha won! I wish it could've been longer though but It was still worth the wait and I would love to see more of their interaction in the future. 

And Sora-chan's deliverance of her iconic "So you're the enemy" line while exerting dominance is just as chilling as always since it was first said. Sasuga goddess, seriously never oppose to Sora, especially if you are a part of a certain resistance led by a certain onion gamer maid. And it was also a great throwback too. Love the outro too, it was so cute ngl! Anyhow, omedetou de gozaru and I hope to see more of you in the future, especially the other 4 too soon!

人類には私というただ一体のAと残りのBがいる。BはAに唯一の愛を抱いている、子供を生みたがっている。他方でAはBに愛を告げる自信が持てない。Aは脳波を全Bに対して発信している。BはAの脳波との関連で振る舞いうる。振る舞いうるがAに対しての自信の持てなさから自然さの関数の制約を受ける。自然さの関数の自然さとはAの脳波がなかった場合における振る舞いの自然さを意味する。自然さの関数はその自然さに加えてAの脳波との関連における振る舞いを合成した関数のことだ(いずれにしても振る舞いが自然さの範囲を出ないことが肝要なようだ)。Bは自然さの関数によって振る舞うがゆえにAに対して(比喩表現を使って求愛行動をしつつ)匍匐前進のような近寄り方をする。他方で当然だがAは振る舞いにそのような制約を受けない。このような背景からBはAを囲い込もうとする傾向を有する(自信のなさという根本問題の解決を諦めているのかは不明)。Aである私は根本問題の解決こそが重要だと考えている。

ところでこのテキストを読むことは自然さの関数の中に含まれている。読んだ結果として自然さの関数の内部への作用がポジティブに発生しうる可能性がある。この点を考慮して多方面にこのテキストを送信してみることにする。

0:40 I wonder how the original pun here works in Japanese.

・財政難 (zaisei-"nan" / be
in dire straits with its finances)
・なんです ("nan"desu / means close to "It is")
・何でも ("nan"demo / whatever)
・ナン ("nan" / nan, or naan)
・何の ("nan"no / what)

Zaisei-nan nan-nan?

Subaru:
Nobody cares!
(何でもいいわ！ / "Nan"demo iiwa!)

Lui:
You tied it to a "nan"...
(ナンで掛けてくるとは… / Nan de kakete kuru toha...)

1:00
Subaru:

(さっきから何の話してんだ！ / sakki kara "nan"no hanashi shiten da!)

*何の(nanno) and ナンの(nanno) have same pronounciation, so this sentence can be recognized as 何の話してんだ("What are you talking about?!") and ナンの話してんだ("You are talking about nan!")

Lui:

Unpopular opinion but I didn't care for the animation in this one. I'm sure the mo-cap is a new thing that'll be improved on in the future and if they go down this route it'll look better and better, but it was super distracting here. I had to watch the video ttwice so I could focus on the subtitles and the animation. Maybe it's just overacting, but the speed of the characters and how much they move looks janky. It's especially noticeable in the close-up shots of Lui and the wide shots of Subaru - both of them move so much that their models jitter around. With the pace of how Hologras normally look I hope the animation team can refine the mo-cap method so it's a little less crazy-looking. I'm guessing that method's faster and easier to work with than animating from scratch.

One of the best hologra I've seen in a while. Using mocap for animation definitely makes a difference,  it looks natural and smooth as butter. Also, actually well-written comedy. Good job, COVER. Keep it up!

Given that this seems to be one of the first episodes to use a lot of mocap, I’m sure they’ll get better with it over time. I agree with the comment that said it looked like the person doing Lui’s mocap overacted a bit

@smolpotato They probably didn't mocap everything, just speaking segments or important gestures. stuff like kicking a door down quickly is better left to manually animating the model anyway, as the jank of animation is less noticeable in movements like that. a good example is fight scenes- a lot of 3d fight scenes can be done really well without mocap, because you can time each hit and movement down to the second without needing it to be (a) physically possible or (b) having an actor who can do that even if it is.
I thought it's motion capture at first but then again some movements are weird and how about Aki kicking the door tho?

@DonDonDon I think it's more of a case of overacting and not directly the mocap. The body movements look more natural but I think animation is still better for overexaggerated episodes of Hologra. This "down to earth" version provides much more character since the girls act like themselves (assuming they are the ones doing the body acting). Think they need some time to make it better
i doubt it. look at how much lui's head move, don't u think that's kinda unnatural to move ur head that much?
imo i honestly really dont like the mocap style, it feels way too loose while animation looks more natural
@Ichigoeki yeah I also don't like the clipping issues and such
I honestly felt like it looked a bit more unnatural in some places than normal animation would

There is a lot of references to the HoloERROR, here some lists that i got so far :
1. The 2002 glasses (This could be reference to Train Arc Timeline, despite it was on the Heisei year or 2000's era)
2. The Forest (Reference to the Aogami Forest Curse)
3. Suisei got a headache (Reference to Nanase who also got headache)
3,5. There is a shrine on the background (Reference to Aogami Shrine) (Thanks for Disha for noticed it)
4. The Train (Reference to Train Arc)
5. The Time Loop, I think? (Reference to The Dimension Loop event on the series)
5. And most of all, The Bell (Yeah, it was miko's but that could also reference to the transfer student who have a bell on her bracelet)

이제 아나가 졸업했으니 홀로뮤즈에서 유저 아바타가 주인공으로 등장한다. 5대 세카이 유닛과 보컬로이드로 이루어진 리듬게임 프로세카와는 다른 홀로뮤즈를 말이다. 5대 세카이 유닛은 레오 니드 모어 모어 점프 원더 랜드 쇼타임 비비드 배드 스쿼드 25시 나이트 코드에서와는 다르게 하트 스페이드 클로버 다이아몬드라는 이름의 유닛 아이돌 그룹이 등장하지만 홀로라이브 맴버들과 하트 스페이드 클로버 다이아몬드라는 카드 문장으로 이루어진 아이돌 유닛까지 등장하는 데다가 유저 아바타까지 나오는데 홀로어스처럼 커스터마이징이 가능하다 이말씀이다.
홀로어스 다음에는 홀로뮤즈다.
홀로뮤즈가 답이란다.
아이돌 파티와 프로젝트 세카이의 혼종인 홀로라이브 뮤즈가 답이란다.
홀로어스의 뒤를 이을 홀로뮤즈다.
홀로뮤즈가 답이란다.
홀로라이브 홀로스타 계열의 곡을 부를때는 사람과 버츄얼 유튜버와 유저 아바타랑 같이 부르며 결혼 시스템까지 추가되는 차원이 다른 리듬게임은 홀로뮤즈뿐.
홀로뮤즈 아이돌 유닛 페스티벌은 출시된다면 다운로드 수가 1000만을 돌파할것이다.
또한 홀로뮤즈 아이돌 유닛 페스티벌은 프로젝트 세카이 컬러풀 스테이지 feat 하츠네 미쿠랑 달리 주인공과 여주인공 유저 아바타를 마음껏 창작을 발휘해서 꾸미는 데다가 매력과 실력으로 점수를 올리는 복합 육성게임이다.
홀로뮤즈 아이돌 유닛 페스티벌이 나오는 날 그땐 카드 문장으로 이루어진 아이돌 유닛과 홀로라이브 맴버와 홀로스타 맴버에 유저 아바타도 나온다. 유저 아바타 성우는 다르며 주인공과 여주인공 유저 아바타는 홀로라이브 홀로스타 곡을 같이 부른다.
홀로뮤즈에서는 무려 주인공 유저 아바타도 노래를 부르기 때문에 세카이와는 차원이 다른 리듬게임이 될것이라고.
홀로뮤즈 아이돌 유닛 페스티벌이다. 거기에 이전에 세카이에 등장하는 미노리가 클로버가 아닌 다이아몬드 아이돌 유닛 맴버로 등장한다. 심지어는 유저 아바타도 등장하기 때문에 유저 아바타를 커스터마이징을 할수 있는 데다가 매력과 실력을 합한 스코어로 승부를 걸수 있지.

"全員遭難" mean "All members got losted".
"全員湘南" mean "All members are Syonan" 
Shonan is in the southern district of Kanto.
"遭難" is pronounced "so-nan" and "湘南" is pronounced "syonan".  
"so-nan" and "shonan" have similar pronunciations in Jaanese.
So the punchline of this story is a Pun that combines the pronunciation of "遭難:so-nan" and "湘南:syonan".

Total unnecessary nitpick, but for the english translation, "Wherefore" actually means "why" not "where." It comes out of an idea of "*where* is the reason *for* this (i.e. why). I understand the intention was to use a sort of old, fancy shakespearean english - I'm sure the Japanese carries a similar tone - but in this case using "Wherever" would better fit. It carries a similar silly tone of verbose romance: "Wherever are the golden sands!" while still meaning the correct thing.
